adoption_plans.executive_summary: Waterloo Public Library shows strong potential for AI-assisted workflow improvement, particularly in follow-up coordination and program documentation. A structured adoption approach starting with a limited pilot and strong human-review processes is recommended. Staff concerns about workload and evaluation have been noted and should be addressed proactively.
current_workflow_context: Manual follow-up email drafting for workshop participants is time-consuming
Program documentation is inconsistent across different staff members
Meeting notes are not consistently captured or shared

opportunities.name: AI-Assisted Program Documentation
opportunities.pain_point: Time-consuming writing and formatting, inconsistent quality
opportunities.possible_ai_support: Generate first drafts from bullet points, notes, or outlines

opportunities.name: Automated Meeting Notes & Action Items
opportunities.pain_point: Inconsistent notes, action items get lost
opportunities.possible_ai_support: Convert rough notes into structured summaries with action items
opportunities.expected_benefit: Consistent follow-up and fewer missed items

recommended_first_pilot.problem_statement: Staff spend significant time drafting follow-up emails after workshops and programs, leading to delayed or inconsistent communication.
recommended_first_pilot.scope_limit: Limited to follow-up emails for one program area with 2-3 participating staff.
success_metrics: Time spent drafting follow-ups per week
Number of edits required per AI draft
stop_or_revise_criteria: AI output quality is consistently poor

roadmap_steps.stage: Validate follow-up pain point with staff
Run pilot with one program area

training_plan.description: How AI assistance works and limitations
Writing effective prompts for email drafting
Evaluating AI output for email content

training_recommendations.note: Training should be tied to the actual pilot workflow, not delivered as generic tool instruction.

items.question: Is there a safe feedback loop?

risks_and_mitigation.risk: Staff may feel AI adds to workload
risks_and_mitigation.mitigation: Set clear expectations that AI is assistive

recommended_next_meeting_questions: Which follow-up tasks take the most staff time each week?
What would make you feel confident in AI-generated email drafts?

pilot_plans.problem_statement: Staff at Waterloo Public Library spend significant time manually drafting follow-up emails after workshops.
pilot_plans.current_process: Staff manually compose individual follow-up emails, searching for past correspondence and program details.
pilot_plans.proposed_ai_assisted_process: AI generates first-draft follow-up emails from program notes. Staff review, edit, and approve before sending.
pilot_plans.scope_limit: Limited to follow-up emails for one program area with 2-3 participating staff.
human_review_checkpoints: Weekly review of AI output quality

pilot_plans.problem_statement: Manual schedule matching and individual confirmation emails for 40+ active volunteers
pilot_plans.current_process: Volunteer scheduling managed through shared spreadsheet. Coordinator spends 4-6 hours per week on schedule matching and email confirmations.
pilot_plans.proposed_ai_assisted_process: AI-assisted schedule matching based on availability, skills, and program needs with automated confirmation draft generation
pilot_plans.scope_limit: Limited to one workflow area and 2-3 participating staff members. No external-facing AI-generated content without human approval.
required_inputs: Sample documents or notes from the selected workflow
Access to knowledge sources used in the workflow
human_review_checkpoints: Before AI output is shared externally
Weekly review of AI output quality by coordinator
privacy_or_quality_risks: AI may generate plausible-sounding but incorrect information
Staff may over-rely on AI output without critical review
Privacy if client data is used in prompts

metrics.name: Time spent on workflow task
metrics.baseline: Measure current time before pilot starts

metrics.name: Output quality / revision rate
metrics.baseline: All outputs are currently manual (100% human effort)
metrics.target: Less than 30% of AI outputs require major revision
metrics.measurement_method: Track revision rate of AI-assisted outputs

metrics.name: Reusable workflow documentation
metrics.target: At least 1 documented workflow shared with the team
metrics.measurement_method: Count of documented and shared workflows

stop_or_revise_criteria: Staff report increased workload instead of reduction
AI output quality is consistently poor despite adjustments
Privacy concerns emerge that cannot be addressed
Staff feedback indicates loss of confidence or trust

training_plan.description: How AI assistance works, its limitations, and responsible use boundaries

training_plan.topic: Prompt Crafting for the Selected Workflow
training_plan.description: How to write effective prompts for Volunteer Schedule Matching and Communication
training_plan.recommended_format: Hands-on session with real workflow examples

training_plan.description: How to critically evaluate AI output, what to check, and when to override
What data can be used in AI prompts, privacy boundaries, and confidentiality requirements

additional_topics.description: Documenting new AI-assisted workflows for team sharing
How to identify and document cases where AI output is incorrect or insufficient

training_recommendations.note: Training should be tied to the actual pilot workflow, not delivered as generic tool instruction. Focus on practical application.

success_metrics.name: Time spent drafting follow-up emails
success_metrics.measurement_method: Self-reported time tracking by participating staff

success_metrics.name: AI draft quality / revision rate
success_metrics.baseline: All drafts are currently manual (100% human effort)
success_metrics.target: Less than 30% of AI-generated drafts require major revision
success_metrics.measurement_method: Track number of AI drafts requiring significant edits

success_metrics.name: Staff confidence with AI-assisted drafting
success_metrics.target: 70% of participating staff report increased confidence
